title: rubus hedycarpus subsp. vulnificus
intro: Rubus hedycarpus subsp. vulnificus, also known as the Western poison oak or poison oak, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant contains a toxic compound called urushiol, which can cause severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. However, in traditional medicine, the plant is used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although it's essential to handle and prepare it with caution due to its toxic properties.
